argument_constraints.tags_any: noun must have at least one of these tags

adjective_fields.modifies: how this adjective shifts noun axis (applied before verb validation)
adjective_fields.constraints: noun must satisfy these before adjective can apply

balance_notes.nouns: Spread across HUMAN, ANIMAL, OBJECT, DEVICE, PLACE, ABSTRACT categories. Abstract/system nouns expanded.
balance_notes.verbs: Spread across PHYSICAL_ACTION, MENTAL_ACTION, SOCIAL_ACTION, SYSTEM_ACTION. System and mental expanded.
balance_notes.adjectives: Constraints distributed across all four axis, not just physicality. New agency/social/system adjective families added.

MAN.description: Adult human male; fully deliberate, embodied, socially embedded

WOMAN.description: Adult human female; fully deliberate, embodied, socially embedded

CHILD.description: Young human; instinct- and emotion-driven, limited deliberate control

ELDER.description: Old human; high social standing, deep relational knowledge, reduced physicality

SOLDIER.description: Armed combatant; goal-directed, trained, operating within a command system

GUARD.description: Protective sentinel; deliberate, physically present, rule-governed role

LEADER.description: One who directs others; maximum agency and social influence

STRANGER.description: Unknown person; fully human but socially disconnected from context

CROWD.description: Mass of people; collective low-deliberation behavior, high social density

DOG.description: Domesticated canine; instinct-driven but highly social and bonded

CAT.description: Domestic feline; instinct-driven, largely solitary, minimally relational

HORSE.description: Large herd animal; powerful, instinct-driven, group-aware

WOLF.description: Pack predator; goal-directed hunting, strong pack hierarchy

INSECT.description: Small invertebrate; mostly reactive, sometimes hive-structured

STONE.description: Dense inorganic solid; completely inert, maximally physical

METAL.description: Refined hard material; inert, elemental, dense

WOOD.description: Organic fibrous material; inert but biologically derived

WATER.description: Fluid substance; shapeless agency, high physical presence

FIRE.description: Combustion process; reactive to conditions, self-propagating pattern

SEED.description: Dormant plant embryo; latent agency, encodes biological pattern

POISON.description: Toxic substance; inert but systemically disruptive, socially dangerous

DUST.description: Fine dispersed particles; minimal form, passive

FOOD.description: Edible matter; inert but socially charged (sustenance, sharing, ritual)

TOOL.description: Shaped object for a purpose; extends human agency, no agency of its own

WEAPON.description: Tool designed for harm; socially loaded, no agency itself

KEY.description: Unlocking artifact; embodies access rights and social permission

BOOK.description: Written record; physical but encodes knowledge, socially transmits ideas

MAP.description: Spatial model of territory; encodes structured spatial knowledge

CAR.description: Motorized vehicle; reactive, mechanical, enables mobility

DRONE.description: Autonomous flying device; semi-autonomous, computationally governed

ROBOT.description: Programmable physical agent; goal-directed, fully computational

ENGINE.description: Power conversion system; reactive, process-governed, high physical output

PHONE.description: Communication device; mediates social interaction, computationally complex

COMPUTER.description: General computation machine; formally governed, processes abstract structures

SENSOR.description: Measurement device; purely reactive, converts physical state to signal

CAMERA.description: Image-capture device; passive observer, socially significant (surveillance/memory)

CLOCK.description: Timekeeping mechanism; process-governed, socially coordinates behavior

NETWORK.description: Connected infrastructure; structural, highly social, self-regulating complexity

CITY.description: Large human settlement; peak social density, rule-governed spatial system

HOME.description: Dwelling space; deeply relational, emotionally and socially core

ROOM.description: Enclosed space; social context-dependent, minimal structure

MARKET.description: Trade space; defined by exchange relationships, rule-governed pricing/norms

PRISON.description: Confinement institution; maximally rule-governed, social control mechanism

FOREST.description: Dense woodland; no human system, minimal social, organic structure

RIVER.description: Flowing waterway; high physical presence, shapes human settlement patterns

MOUNTAIN.description: Massive landform; maximum physicality, culturally/socially significant

BORDER.description: Boundary between territories; mostly conceptual, huge social/political weight

BRIDGE.description: Crossing structure; physical but socially connecting

RULE.description: Normative constraint; no physical form, governs social behavior through system

LAW.description: Formal social rule with enforcement; civilization-level social, formally codified

SYSTEM.description: Set of interacting parts; purely structural, maximally systemic

PATTERN.description: Repeating structure; no agency or social dimension, purely formal

PLAN.description: Intended sequence of actions; encodes agency but is not itself an agent

TRUTH.description: Correspondence to reality; socially fundamental, weakly systemic (logic)

LIE.description: False assertion; purely social artifact, low systemic structure

POWER.description: Capacity to cause change; latent agency, fundamentally social

FEAR.description: Emotional state of threat-response; experienced mentally, socially communicated

MEMORY.description: Stored past experience; no form, process-governed retrieval

SIGNAL.description: Information-carrying difference; formally structured, socially interpreted

ERROR.description: Deviation from expected state; purely systemic, no social or physical form

CHOICE.description: Selection between alternatives; inherently agentive, socially consequential

DEBT.description: Obligation between agents; socially binding, formally tracked

IDENTITY.description: Sense of self; agentive in expression, defined through social recognition

ALGORITHM.description: Formal procedure; maximally systemic, asocial, no physical form

LANGUAGE.description: Symbolic communication system; civilization-level social, formally structured

TIME.description: Dimension of change; not physical, not social, but structures both

CHANCE.description: Unpredictable outcome; minimal structure, undermines deterministic systems

RUN.description: Move fast under own power; instinct-sufficient, requires full embodiment

FALL.description: Move downward under gravity; no agency required, physical mass sufficient

EAT.description: Ingest solid matter; requires biological embodiment and instinct-level drive

BUILD.description: Assemble into something new; requires deliberate intent and physical embodiment

BREAK.description: Damage physical integrity; requires force, target must have physical form

MOVE.description: Displace from one location to another; minimal agency needed, physical form required

CARRY.description: Transport while bearing weight; requires physical strength and intent

BURN.description: Consume by fire; can happen without agency, requires physical matter

GROW.description: Increase in size or complexity over time; biological or systemic process

SEE.description: Detect visually; passive perception requiring physical eyes or sensors

HEAR.description: Detect acoustically; passive perception, can pick up abstract signals

THINK.description: Form thoughts deliberately; requires highest agency, biological embodiment

KNOW.description: Hold stable belief as knowledge; requires social mind to define truth

CHOOSE.description: Select between alternatives; requires deliberate agency, can affect social outcomes

IMAGINE.description: Create mental representation of non-present things; uniquely high-agency, purely mental

FORGET.description: Lose access to stored information; partly involuntary, biologically grounded

WANT.description: Desire something; instinct-level or deliberate, no physical form needed

TRUST.description: Rely on another agent despite uncertainty; high-agency social judgment

TALK.description: Produce speech to communicate; requires deliberate agency, social context

ASK.description: Request information or action from another; deliberate, directed social act

WARN.description: Alert another to danger; deliberate social act with protective intent

HELP.description: Assist another's goals; requires recognition of another's needs

BETRAY.description: Violate trust deliberately; deeply agentive, purely social harm

ATTACK.description: Initiate violence against; requires physical or social power, directed intent

FOLLOW.description: Move after or obey; social submission or physical tracking

TRADE.description: Exchange goods or favors; requires mutual recognition, social norms, systemic context

GENERATE.description: Produce output from formal process; systemic, not requiring physical form

COMPUTE.description: Execute formal operations on symbols; purely systemic, asocial

FAIL.description: Cease to function as expected; purely systemic event, no agency involved

UPDATE.description: Replace old state with new; computational or rule-governed process

ENCODE.description: Convert information into a formal representation; purely systemic transformation

SPREAD.description: Propagate across a network or population; social or systemic diffusion

BIG.description: Greater than normal in size; applies to physical things only

SMALL.description: Less than normal in size; applies to physical things only

HEAVY.description: High mass relative to expectations; strongly physical modifier

DENSE.description: Compact and heavy per volume; resists processing, very physical

VAST.description: Enormous in scale; applies to places and systems, carries social weight

STRONG.description: High physical or agentive force; applies to embodied things

WEAK.description: Low force or capacity; reduces both agency and physicality

FAST.description: High rate of action or processing; boosts agency and systemic throughput

SLOW.description: Low rate of action; inhibits agency and systemic speed

SHARP.description: Precise edge or mind; physical sharpness or cognitive precision

DULL.description: Blunt or cognitively slow; reduces physical precision and systemic clarity

CLEAN.description: Free of contamination; socially acceptable, well-ordered

DIRTY.description: Contaminated or disordered; socially rejected, reduces systemic integrity

HAPPY.description: Experiencing positive affect; boosts social engagement and motivation

SAD.description: Experiencing negative affect; reduces social drive and motivation

ANGRY.description: High-arousal negative emotion; spikes agency but disrupts social bonds

CALM.description: Low arousal, stable state; improves social and rational function

PROUD.description: Self-regarding positive emotion; requires social comparison, high deliberation

ASHAMED.description: Social self-evaluation failure; collapses social standing and agency

FREE.description: Unconstrained by external control; maximizes agentive latitude

TRAPPED.description: Unable to act or escape; severely reduces agency and social access

OBEDIENT.description: Following commands; trades agency for social acceptance and systemic fit

REBELLIOUS.description: Actively defying norms; high agency, rejects social and systemic order

DEAD.description: Biologically ceased; annihilates all agency and most social function

BROKEN.description: Structurally failed; reduces functional agency and systemic integrity

ACTIVE.description: Currently operating; boosts agency and systemic engagement

DORMANT.description: Inactive but capable of reactivation; suppressed agency

ISOLATED.description: Cut off from social contact; collapses social dimension

CONNECTED.description: Linked to others or to network; amplifies social and systemic reach

TRUSTED.description: Judged reliable by others; strong social endorsement

OUTCAST.description: Rejected by community; near-total social exclusion

FAMOUS.description: Known widely across society; maximum social reach

SECRET.description: Hidden from social knowledge; low social visibility, high informational structure

COMPLEX.description: High internal structure; applies to systems, rules, and devices

SIMPLE.description: Low internal structure; reduces systemic complexity

ORDERED.description: Internally organized; increases systemic and social regularity

CHAOTIC.description: Lacking order; collapses systemic structure and social predictability

ENCRYPTED.description: Formally protected information; highly structured, socially opaque

CORRUPTED.description: Internal integrity damaged; destroys systemic function and social trust

AUTOMATED.description: Self-executing without manual input; elevates systemic autonomy

OPTIMAL.description: Best possible outcome for given constraints; formal evaluation, systemic goal
